records.ety: [Cf. It. credenziale, fr. LL. credentia. See Credence.]

defs: Giving a title or claim to credit or confidence; accrediting.
quotes: Their credential letters on both sides.

defs: That which gives a title to credit or confidence.

Testimonials showing that a person is entitled to credit, or has right to exercise official power, as the letters given by a government to an ambassador or envoy, or a certificate that one is a duly elected delegate.
quotes: The committee of estates excepted against the credentials of the English commissioners.
Had they not shown undoubted credentials from the Divine Person who sent them on such a message.
